title: Spin resolved spectral topology and re-entrant localization in a non Hermitian quasiperiodic SSH chain
abstract: We investigate localization and spectral topology in a non Hermitian quasiperiodic Su Schrieffer Heeger lattice with Rashba spin orbit coupling and spin-dependent hopping. By analyzing the inverse participation ratio, complex energy spectrum, and spectral winding numbers, we demonstrate the emergence of a re-entrant transition from extended to localized and back to extended phases as the non-Hermitian parameter increases. The localization transition is accompanied by a simultaneous real-complex-real spectral transition in the complex-energy plane. In the absence of spin dependent hopping, the spectrum forms two nearly spin-degenerate loops characterized by winding numbers w = 2. Upon introducing finite spin-dependent hopping, each loop splits into two independent spin-resolved spectral branches, resulting in four disconnected spectral contours carrying distinct winding sectors. Our results reveal a direct correspondence between localization, spectral topology, and spin-resolved spectral splitting in non-Hermitian quasiperiodic systems.
summary: This paper explores how non-Hermiticity and spin-dependent hopping affect localization and topology in a quasiperiodic SSH chain. It reveals a unique re-entrant phase transition where the system moves from extended to localized and back to extended states. The study also shows that spin-dependent hopping can split spectral loops into distinct, spin-resolved topological branches.
main_problem: The study investigates the interplay between non-Hermiticity, quasiperiodicity, Rashba spin-orbit coupling, and spin-dependent hopping in determining localization and spectral topology.
main_result: The authors demonstrate a re-entrant transition from extended to localized and back to extended phases, which is accompanied by a simultaneous real-complex-real spectral transition and spin-resolved spectral splitting.
method: The study uses exact diagonalization of the Hamiltonian to analyze the complex energy spectrum and localization properties.
model_or_system: A non-Hermitian quasiperiodic Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain featuring Rashba spin-orbit coupling and spin-dependent hopping, modeled via a generalized Aubry-Andre-Harper lattice.
key_observables: Inverse Participation Ratio (IPR), Normalized Participation Ratio (NPR), spectral winding numbers (w), and the fraction of complex eigenvalues.
important_parameters: Non-Hermitian parameter (h), spin-dependent hopping strength (g_h), Rashba spin-orbit coupling (alpha), and quasiperiodic modulation strength (lambda).
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows IPR/NPR vs h demonstrating re-entrant localization; Fig 2 presents phase diagrams for localization (E, I, L) and spectral phases (R, M, C); Fig 4 shows the complex energy spectrum evolution and the splitting of spectral loops due to spin-dependent hopping.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the non-Hermitian SSH model, details the numerical methodology, presents the results regarding re-entrant localization and spectral transitions, analyzes the impact of spin-dependent hopping on topology, and concludes with potential experimental implementations.
why_interesting: It explores the complex interplay of non-Hermiticity and spin-orbit coupling, providing insights into how non-Hermitian driving can induce re-entrant phase transitions and controllable spin-resolved topological phases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Here δ1 means all parameter except v ,w h,α are zero δ2 include α1 = 1, α2 = 2 δ3 further have η = 1 and δ4 also include α3 = 1, α4 = 0.5 L = 1597.

FIG. 2. (a) Phase diagram in the α–h plane obtained from the average inverse participation ratio, ⟨IPR⟩, showing the extended (E), intermediate (I), and localized (L) phases. The color map represents the magnitude of ⟨IPR⟩. (b) Phase dia- gram in the α–h plane constructed from the density of states ρ, distinguishing the PT -unbroken (R), mixed (C), and PT - broken (B) phases. L = 610

FIG. 3. Spin-resolved inverse participation ratio (IPR) as a function of Re(E) for different values of the non-Hermitian parameter h and spin-dependent hopping gh. Red and blue dots correspond to the spin-up and spin-down sectors, respec- tively.L = 1597

FIG. 4. Complex-energy spectrum in the complex-energy plane colored by the inverse participation ratio (IPR). Panels (a,b) compare the spectra for gh = 0 and gh = 1 at fixed h = 4, showing the splitting of spectral loops due to spin-dependent hopping. Panels (c–h) show the evolution of the spectrum with increasing non-Hermitian strength h for gh = 1, demonstrating the real-to-complex-to-real spectral transition accompanied by localization and re-entrant delocalization behavior. L = 1597

Figure 5 summarizes the evolution of the spectral winding numbers together with the localization proper- ties of the system. The upper panels display the real part of the spectrum as a function of the non-Hermitian parameter h, where the color scale denotes the inverse participation ratio (IPR). The lower panels show the cor- responding winding numbers associated with the discon- nected spectral contours.
